step_by_step_thinking:

Although the celiac trunk serves many important organs, its injury might not immediately cause life-threatening conditions because there can often be collateral circulation from adjacent regions supplying some parts of the stomach and proximal bowel. This means that even though it’s critically important anatomically speaking, functional compromise due to isolated celiac artery injury may present later rather acutely compared to direct insults to solitary high-priority vascular territories like the renal arteries.
